chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Reward of Visiting the Prophet {saws} and the Imams {a.s}, Peace Be Upon Them All

text: 3176 - وَ قَالَ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَدْنَى مَا يُثَابُ بِهِ زَائِرُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِشَطِّ اَلْفُرَاتِ إِذَا عَرَفَ حَقَّهُ وَ حُرْمَتَهُ وَ وَلاَيَتَهُ أَنْ يُغْفَرَ لَهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِهِ وَ مَا تَأَخَّرَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3176 - Imam Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
"The least reward for the visitor of Abu Abdullah (Imam Hussain ibn Ali (as)) at the banks of the Euphrates, if they recognize his right, sanctity, and authority, is that their past and future sins will be forgiven."
